subtasks.description: Generate SQL query to identify top 10 TPC-H customers by lifetime value (total order amount) in market segment 'BUILDING'. Return customer_id, customer_name, and total_lifetime_value.

Generate SQL query to retrieve detailed order patterns for the identified top 10 customers: order frequency, order dates, average order value, order count by month, and largest/smallest orders. Include temporal and behavioral dimensions.

Reconcile top 10 customer LTV data with their order patterns. For each customer, synthesize a personalized retention narrative that references their order pattern (frequency, seasonal trends, order magnitude), lifetime relationship duration, and segment value. Generate 10 distinct narratives with customer-specific language.

plan.cost_rationale: Three subtasks, minimal decomposition: (1) sql_gen_ltv (mid-tier) identifies top 10 with a straightforward aggregation and segment filter; (2) sql_gen_order_patterns (mid-tier) retrieves rich behavioral data with window functions or date grouping but is still a single focused query; (3) cross_recon (frontier-tier) performs the heavy synthesis—it must synthesize 10 personalized narratives by correlating LTV rankings with multi-dimensional order patterns and generating contextual, customer-specific language. Frontier tier is justified because narrative generation requires inference, contextual understanding of customer behavior, and high-quality synthesis across two data sources. The plan avoids unnecessary policy_action (no compliance requirement) and keeps subtasks to the minimum needed to deliver the full answer.

output.sql: SELECT c.c_custkey AS customer_id, c.c_name AS customer_name, SUM(o.o_totalprice) AS total_lifetime_value FROM customer c INNER JOIN orders o ON c.c_custkey = o.o_custkey WHERE c.c_mktsegment = 'BUILDING' GROUP BY c.c_custkey, c.c_name ORDER BY total_lifetime_value DESC LIMIT 10

WITH top_customers AS (SELECT c.c_custkey AS customer_id, c.c_name AS customer_name, SUM(o.o_totalprice) AS total_lifetime_value FROM customer c INNER JOIN orders o ON c.c_custkey = o.o_custkey WHERE c.c_mktsegment = 'BUILDING' GROUP BY c.c_custkey, c.c_name ORDER BY total_lifetime_value DESC LIMIT 10) SELECT tc.customer_id, tc.customer_name, tc.total_lifetime_value, o.o_orderkey, o.o_orderdate, o.o_totalprice, o.o_orderstatus, o.o_orderpriority, COUNT(*) OVER (PARTITION BY tc.customer_id) AS total_order_count, AVG(o.o_totalprice) OVER (PARTITION BY tc.customer_id) AS avg_order_value, MAX(o.o_totalprice) OVER (PARTITION BY tc.customer_id) AS largest_order_value, MIN(o.o_totalprice) OVER (PARTITION BY tc.customer_id) AS smallest_order_value, TO_CHAR(o.o_orderdate, 'YYYY-MM') AS order_month, COUNT(*) OVER (PARTITION BY tc.customer_id, TO_CHAR(o.o_orderdate, 'YYYY-MM')) AS orders_per_month, RANK() OVER (PARTITION BY tc.customer_id ORDER BY o.o_orderdate) AS order_sequence FROM top_customers tc INNER JOIN orders o ON tc.customer_id = o.o_custkey ORDER BY tc.total_lifetime_value DESC, tc.customer_id, o.o_orderdate
